engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru Legacy Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru Legacy in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data for grey import specialists.

cards.altText: Subaru Legacy EJ20 engine - petrol 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - MLS gasket degradation from turbo heat cycles on high-mileage units.
Timing belt failure - interference engine risks catastrophic valve damage if belt snaps.
Oil starvation - turbo variants suffer bearing wear if oil changes are delayed.

cards.altText: Subaru Legacy EJ25 engine - petrol 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - notorious EJ251/EJ253 gasket weakness causing coolant/oil mixing.
Piston ring wear - oil consumption increases after 70k miles on higher-mileage imports.
Timing belt failure - critical service interval at 60k miles to prevent interference damage.

cards.altText: Subaru Legacy EZ30 engine - petrol 3.0L flat-six replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - cold start rattle on high-mileage units if service intervals missed.
Oil leak from cam covers - flat-six design complicates access for seal replacement.
Coolant pipe degradation - plastic pipes crack with age causing overheating risk.
cards.replacementCost: Typical UK rebuilt engine: £2,400-£4,500 (supply only) Estimated from comparable EZ30 units - actual quotes may vary.

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Core Legacy engine across all generations; versatile boxer design powering entry and performance trims.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure on turbo variants from heat cycle stress causing coolant/oil mixing
Timing belt failure on interference design - catastrophic valve damage if not replaced by 60k miles
Oil starvation in turbo models leading to bearing wear if service intervals missed
entries.cta: Get quotes for EJ20 Subaru Legacy engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Naturally Aspirated
entries.history: Base EJ20 variant for entry-level Legacy models; reliable SOHC design.
commonFailures: Head gasket weakness shared with EJ family - monitor for coolant loss
Timing belt service critical at 60k miles to prevent interference damage

entries.title: 2.0L Naturally Aspirated (Revised)
entries.history: Updated EJ201 with minor revisions for improved reliability on later Legacy models.
commonFailures: Reduced head gasket issues vs early EJ201 but timing belt remains critical

entries.history: Top-spec EJ20 turbo with forged internals for Spec-B and enthusiast applications.

Head gasket and turbo wear remain primary concerns

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: Larger-displacement boxer for enhanced torque; fitted to 2.5i and 2.5GT variants.

Piston ring wear causing oil consumption on high-mileage imports

entries.history: Base EJ25 variant; most common 2.5L engine in UK Legacy imports.

commonProblems.h2: Subaru Legacy Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru Legacy relies primarily on EJ20 and EJ25 naturally aspirated and turbocharged boxer engines across most UK variants, with the rare EZ30 flat-six and EE20 diesel options. While generally robust, head gasket failure on EJ20/EJ25, timing belt neglect, and turbo wear dominate replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: EJ20/EJ25 Head Gasket Failure - Why Subaru Legacy Engines Leak Coolant
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 70,000-110,000 miles on high-mileage grey imports with incomplete service history
problems.rootCause: Multi-layer steel gasket degrades from thermal cycling; turbo variants suffer accelerated failure from exhaust heat. Coolant mixes with oil, causing overheating and potential engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, fit upgraded MLS gasket, resurface if needed, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block/head undamaged; requires Subaru boxer specialist knowledge

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EJ20/EJ25 with Upgraded Gasket
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded MLS gasket, full service, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating occurred or mileage exceeds 90k; peace of mind for turbo owners

problems.recommendation: If your Legacy shows only minor seepage with no overheating history, gasket replacement at £600-£950 may suffice. However, if overheating occurred or mileage is high, a rebuilt EJ20/EJ25 at £1,200-£2,800 avoids repeat failures and protects your investment.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EJ20/EJ25 engine prices for your Subaru Legacy

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Risk on Subaru Legacy Interference Engines
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles if belt not replaced per schedule
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belt degrades with age and heat; if it snaps on interference-design boxer, pistons strike valves causing catastrophic internal damage requiring full replacement.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing belt, tensioner, idlers, water pump before failure occurs
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic damage; recommended every 60k miles or 5 years

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new timing kit, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after belt failure; restores vehicle to reliable condition

problems.recommendation: On a Legacy worth £2,000-£5,000, preventative belt replacement at £350-£650 offers exceptional value. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt EJ20/EJ25 at £1,200-£2,800 is the only viable solution - attempting valve repair rarely makes economic sense.
problems.cta: Compare timing-belt-upgraded Subaru Legacy engine prices

problems.h4: EJ20 Turbo Failure - Boost Loss & Oil Consumption on Legacy GT/GTB
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 80,000-120,000 miles on enthusiast-driven or poorly maintained grey imports
problems.rootCause: Turbocharger bearings wear from heat and oil starvation; degraded seals cause boost loss, blue smoke, and eventual seizure if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, rebuild or replace with OEM-spec unit, oil feed line inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Restores boost if engine internals undamaged; requires turbo specialist

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EJ20 Turbo Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EJ20 turbo with new turbo, upgraded gaskets, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure caused oil contamination or engine damage

problems.recommendation: If your 2.0GT shows only boost loss with no knocking or oil contamination, turbo replacement at £700-£1,300 may suffice. However, if oil contamination occurred or mileage is high, a rebuilt EJ20 turbo at £1,800-£2,800 avoids repeat failures.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EJ20 turbo engine prices for your Subaru Legacy

problems.h4: EZ30 Timing Chain Tensioner Wear - Cold Start Rattle on Legacy 3.0R
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 90,000-130,000 miles if oil changes delayed or incorrect viscosity used
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure on cold start; rattle indicates chain slap that can damage guides if ignored, leading to timing jump and valve damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides, chain inspection, oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if caught early; prevents secondary damage to sprockets

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EZ30 with Revised Tensioner
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EZ30 with updated tensioner, full timing service, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if rattle accompanied by performance loss or high mileage

problems.recommendation: If your 3.0R shows only cold-start rattle with no performance loss, tensioner replacement at £550-£950 is sensible. However, on grey imports over 100k miles, upgrading to a rebuilt EZ30 with revised components often proves better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for timing-chain-upgraded Subaru Legacy 3.0R engine replacement

problems.h4: EE20 DPF & EGR Issues - Emissions Compliance on Subaru Legacy Diesel
problems.typicalFailureMileage: 60,000-90,000 miles on short-journey UK driving patterns
problems.rootCause: Boxer diesel DPF requires sustained high-speed driving to regenerate; UK urban use causes clogging, EGR carbon buildup, MOT failures.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-car DPF clean, EGR valve replacement, ECU adaptation, emissions test
repairOptions.longevity: Restores MOT compliance if engine internals sound; requires ongoing motorway use

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EE20 with UK-spec emissions components, full documentation, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Guaranteed MOT pass; ideal for grey import diesel Legacy owners seeking compliance certainty

problems.recommendation: For diesel Legacy owners facing MOT failure, a rebuilt EE20 with UK-spec emissions components at £1,600-£3,000 provides peace of mind and avoids repeated testing costs, especially given the rarity of EE20 parts in the UK.
problems.cta: Compare MOT-compliant Subaru Legacy diesel engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru Legacy
finalCta.paragraph: The Subaru Legacy's boxer engines are robust when maintained, but grey import status, age and head gasket vulnerability mean failures can be costly. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK specialists who understand Legacy compatibility, chassis codes (BL/BP/BM/BR), turbo adaptation and MOT compliance. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing original failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare quotes today and get your Legacy back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru Legacy Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru Legacy and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Subaru Legacy Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Subaru Legacy is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes for UK grey import owners.

items.title: Subaru Legacy 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0L EJ20 naturally aspirated and turbocharged petrol boxer engine powered most Subaru Legacy variants sold as grey imports to the UK from 2003-2014. This versatile aluminium-block DOHC unit with AVCS variable valve timing delivered 140-280hp depending on trim, though head gasket vulnerability on turbo variants requires diligent maintenance.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on turbo variants
timing belt failure risk on interference design
oil starvation on turbo models if service intervals missed
items.cta: Compare Subaru Legacy 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru Legacy 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5L EJ25 naturally aspirated and turbocharged petrol boxer engine offered enhanced torque for Legacy 2.5i and 2.5GT variants. While providing stronger low-end pull than the 2.0L, the EJ25 is notorious for head gasket weakness on early revisions, making preventative maintenance and upgraded gasket specification critical for long-term reliability on grey imports.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure notorious on EJ251/EJ253
piston ring wear causing oil consumption
items.cta: Get quotes for Subaru Legacy 2.5L petrol engines

items.title: Subaru Legacy 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L EZ30 horizontally-opposed six-cylinder petrol engine powered the flagship Legacy 3.0R and Spec-B variants, delivering smooth, refined 245hp performance. While generally robust, the flat-six design complicates access for maintenance, and timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage units requires specialist attention to prevent costly secondary damage.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear causing cold start rattle
oil leaks from cam cover seals
items.cta: Compare Subaru Legacy 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru Legacy 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L EE20 horizontally-opposed diesel engine was Subaru's unique boxer diesel offering, fitted to rare Legacy 2.0D grey imports. While efficient, the EE20 requires sustained high-speed driving for DPF regeneration and is sensitive to service intervals; UK urban use patterns can accelerate emissions system failures.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain stretch on high-mileage units

Strong donor parts availability via shared Subaru Legacy/Outback platform

faq.h2: Subaru Legacy Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru Legacy engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru Legacy engine replacement costs range from £600 for Japanese used imports to £4,500 for UK reconditioned EZ30 flat-six units, depending on engine code, variant and supplier. Supply & fit services add £550-£950 labour.
keyPoints: Japanese used EJ20 engines start from £600 with auction grade verification
UK reconditioned EJ20/EJ25 units range £1,200-£2,800 with 12-month warranty
EZ30 flat-six and turbo variants typically cost £400-£800 more than naturally aspirated
Supply & fit labour adds £550-£950 depending on specialist location and AWD/turbo complexity
items.cta: Compare Subaru Legacy engine replacement quotes from vetted UK specialists

items.question: What engine code fits my Subaru Legacy?
items.answer: Most Subaru Legacy variants use EJ20 or EJ25 2.0-2.5L naturally aspirated or turbocharged petrol boxer engine codes, but chassis codes BL/BP (2003-2009) and BM/BR (2009-2014) determine compatibility with your vehicle's mounting points and emissions systems.
keyPoints: EJ20 is the dominant engine code across 2.0i, 2.0GT and GT variants
Verify chassis code BL/BP for pre-facelift or BM/BR for facelift compatibility before ordering
Enter your registration above for instant DVLA-verified engine code confirmation
EZ30 flat-six and rare EE20 diesel codes require specialist verification for correct replacement matching
items.cta: Verify your Subaru Legacy EJ20 engine code instantly via DVLA lookup

items.question: Is a reconditioned Subaru Legacy engine better than a used Japanese import?
items.answer: Reconditioned EJ20/EJ25 engines offer UK warranty coverage and upgraded components addressing known failures like head gasket weakness, while used Japanese imports provide lower upfront cost but limited warranty and potential compliance documentation gaps.
keyPoints: Reconditioned units include 12-month UK warranty vs 3-6 months for Japanese imports
Rebuilt EJ20/EJ25 engines feature upgraded head gaskets addressing Legacy failure patterns
Japanese imports require additional IVA/MOT documentation verification for UK compliance
Budget-conscious owners may prefer imports; long-term owners benefit from reconditioned reliability
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs used Subaru Legacy engine options with warranty details

items.question: Will a replacement engine affect my Subaru Legacy UK MOT or registration?
items.answer: Replacement engines must meet UK emissions standards and include proper documentation for DVLA registration; EngineMarket specialists supply EJ20/EJ25/EZ30 units with Euro 4 certification and IVA support for grey import compliance.
keyPoints: All replacement EJ20/EJ25 engines include UK-spec catalytic converter for MOT emissions compliance
Emissions certification documentation provided for DVLA registration updates on grey imports
IVA test support included for Legacy vehicles requiring Individual Vehicle Approval
Specialists handle ECU immobiliser adaptation and turbo calibration for seamless UK registration transfer
items.cta: Find MOT-compliant Subaru Legacy engine replacements with full documentation

items.question: How long does Subaru Legacy engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: UK reconditioned EJ20/EJ25 engines typically dispatch within 2 days; Japanese imports require 10-14 days delivery plus 1 day fitting. Supply & fit bookings can often be scheduled within 48 hours for in-stock units.
keyPoints: In-stock UK reconditioned units: 2-day supply & fit turnaround typical for EJ20 variants
Japanese import engines: 10-14 days delivery plus 1 day specialist fitting with chassis verification
Turbo and AWD variants may require additional time for adaptation service and drivetrain compatibility checks
Emergency replacement helpline available for seized engine scenarios with priority dispatch from UK stock
items.cta: Check live Subaru Legacy engine stock availability and delivery timelines

items.question: Can I fit a Subaru Legacy engine myself to save money?
items.answer: DIY engine fitting is not recommended for Subaru Legacy grey imports due to boxer engine-specific tools, turbo adaptation requirements, AWD drivetrain calibration and UK compliance documentation best handled by specialists.
keyPoints: EJ20/EJ25 boxer engines require specific lifting equipment and timing tools for safe installation
Turbo variants need ECU adaptation service - specialist tooling essential for boost calibration
IVA/MOT documentation support requires specialist knowledge of grey import compliance procedures
Supply & fit packages often cost less than DIY when factoring tool hire, risk of errors and warranty validity
items.cta: Book Subaru Legacy engine supply & fit with grey import specialist network

items.question: What are the most common Subaru Legacy engine problems?
items.answer: EJ20/EJ25 head gasket failure from thermal cycling, timing belt neglect on interference-design boxer engines, and turbo wear on GT/GTB variants dominate Subaru Legacy replacement enquiries from UK grey import owners.
keyPoints: Head gasket failure affects early EJ251/EJ253 revisions; rebuilt units include upgraded MLS gaskets as standard
Timing belt must be replaced every 60k miles to prevent catastrophic valve damage on interference engines
Turbo variants require diligent oil change intervals to prevent bearing wear and boost loss
All rebuilt EJ20/EJ25 units include upgraded components addressing these known grey import failure points
items.cta: Compare Subaru Legacy engine replacements with failure-proof upgraded components

items.question: Is it worth repairing or replacing my Subaru Legacy engine?
items.answer: If repair costs exceed 60% of a reconditioned EJ20 price (£1,200-£2,800), replacement offers better long-term value with UK warranty coverage and upgraded components addressing original failure patterns.
keyPoints: Minor repairs (head gasket, turbo rebuild) cost £600-£1,300 and may be viable for low-mileage units
Major failures (timing belt snap, overheating damage) typically require full replacement for reliability
Rebuilt EJ20/EJ25 engines include 12-month warranty vs limited coverage on repaired original units
Consider vehicle value: Legacy grey imports typically worth £2,000-£5,000 in UK market
items.cta: Get instant repair vs replace cost comparison for your Subaru Legacy variant

items.question: Does the Subaru Legacy engine work with other Subaru donor parts?
items.answer: Yes - the EJ20 engine was also fitted to Subaru Impreza, Forester and Outback in other markets, expanding UK parts availability and reducing lead times for grey import Legacy owners seeking replacement components.
keyPoints: EJ20 cross-compatibility increases donor pool for hard-to-find Legacy-specific components
Timing kits, gaskets and sensors often available from Subaru Impreza/Forester suppliers in UK
Verify part numbers match your Legacy chassis code (BL/BP/BM/BR) and variant before ordering
EngineMarket specialists source from cross-platform Subaru inventory to reduce wait times
